item.description:
## DuoUpdater 0.1.7
**iPhone/iPad apps on Mac now redirect to the App Store.** Wrapped iOS apps running on Apple Silicon (e.g. Aqara Home) can only be updated by the App Store app itself — the `mas` command-line tool has no Mac-store entry for them and fails with "No apps found for ADAM ID …". These apps no longer show a one-click Update that errors out; instead the row offers an **App Store** button that opens the app's page, where the real Update lives. They're also excluded from Install-All so a batch can't stall on them.

## DuoUpdater 0.1.6
**Resilient downloads.** Large installer downloads now survive a dropped connection. If a transfer is cut off mid-stream — a flaky network, a VPN/proxy that resets long downloads — DuoUpdater retries and resumes from where it left off (HTTP range requests) instead of restarting from zero or giving up with "The network connection was lost." This fixes repeated update failures on big payloads (e.g. Cursor, Codex).
**Snappier update checks.** Resolving your GitHub token now happens off the main thread with a short timeout, so a slow `gh` CLI can't stall a refresh.

## DuoUpdater 0.1.5
- Released: 2026-06-22 - Build: 6 - Package: notarized macOS app bundle - Install: download `DuoUpdater-0.1.5-macos.zip`, unzip it, move `DuoUpdater.app` to `/Applications`, then open it once.
SHA-256: `3515cead4c9c1a6e477e2a9b212c110a7121ce39fb4f2e32520faae415e628a6`
